message.content:
Review the quality of a new TypeScript utility file for a personal CV Astro site.

WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: Created src/utils/cv-html.ts — a new TypeScript file exporting getCvHtml(): string that returns a complete self-contained HTML document (inline CSS + full CV content) used as the single source of truth for PDF generation.

PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task 2 from docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-13-cv-upgrade.md in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro

BASE_SHA: 70d158f
HEAD_SHA: daeeff7

DESCRIPTION: New utility file. Single exported function. No tests applicable (pure template string, no logic). Review for: TypeScript quality, template structure clarity, no broken HTML in the template string, appropriate code organisation.

Work from: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro

content.text: I'll start by reading the plan document and the implemented file, along with checking the git diff.

content.content:
1 # CV Upgrade Implementation Plan 2 3 > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. 4 5 **Goal:** Rewrite CV content with ATS keywords and quantified achievements, create a clean professional PDF template, and wire it to the existing download endpoint. 6 7 **Architecture:** `src/utils/cv-html.ts` exports a single `getCvHtml()` function that returns a complete self-contained HTML document. `src/pages/cv-pdf.astro` renders it as a browser-previewable page. `src/pages/andrii-popenko-cv.js` passes the same HTML to `html-pdf-node`. `src/content/list/cv.md` is updated separately for the dark-themed website. 8 9 **Tech Stack:** Astro 4, TypeScript, html-pdf-node, Tailwind (website only — PDF template uses inline CSS) 10 11 --- 12 13 ## File Map 14 15 | File | Action | Responsibility | 16 |------|--------|----------------| 17 | `src/content/list/cv.md` | Modify | Website CV content (dark theme, markdown) | 18 | `src/utils/cv-html.ts` | Create | Self-contained HTML+CSS string for ATS-safe PDF | 19 | `src/pages/cv-pdf.astro` | Create | Browser preview of the clean PDF template | 20 | `src/pages/andrii-popenko-cv.js` | Modify | Replace temp/index.html read with getCvHtml() | 21 22 `src/components/Header.astro` — no changes needed, download link already present. 23 24 --- 25 26 ## Task 1: Rewrite cv.md for the website 27 28 **Files:** 29 - Modify: `src/content/list/cv.md` 30 31 - [ ] **Step 1: Replace the entire file content** 32 33 ```markdown 34 ### Andrii Popenko. content.text:
All checks pass. Here is the full review.

---

## Code Review: `src/utils/cv-html.ts` — Task 2

**Overall verdict: PASS. The implementation is a faithful, clean execution of the plan with no blocking issues and two minor improvement suggestions.**

---

### Plan Alignment

The implementation matches the plan specification exactly:

- File created at the correct path `src/utils/cv-html.ts`.
- Single named export `getCvHtml(): string` — matches the plan signature.
- Returns a complete, self-contained `<!DOCTYPE html>` document with inline CSS.
- No external dependencies or imports introduced.
- Commit message `"feat: add cv-html template utility for ATS-compliant PDF"` matches the plan's prescribed message precisely.
- All six required CV sections are present: Professional Summary, Core Competencies, Experience (8 roles), Technical Skills, Education, Languages.
- Content is identical to the cv.md rewrite from Task 1, correctly adapted to HTML form.

No deviations from the plan were found.

---

### TypeScript Quality

The file is correct TypeScript. The `astro/tsconfigs/strict` extension is in use (which sets `strict: true`), and this file has no type-level concerns — a pure template literal returning `string` is the correct, maximally simple approach. The explicit `: string` return type annotation is present and appropriate; it serves as a contract for the callers in Tasks 3 and 4.

No issues.

---

### HTML Template Integrity

Automated structural checks confirm:

- All HTML tags are correctly balanced — no unclosed or mismatched elements.
- All ampersands are properly entity-encoded (`&`, `–`, `—`, `·`, `’`, `“`, `”`). No bare `&` characters.
- No unescaped backticks or `${}` template expressions that could corrupt the template literal at runtime.
- The `<title>` uses a literal em-dash (`—`) rather than `—` — this is valid inside `<title>` and will render correctly.

---

### CSS / Rendering Quality

The CSS is well-structured for a PDF context:

- Reset (`* { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }`) is appropriate.
- `body` uses `max-width: 21cm` and `padding: 2cm 2.2cm` which correctly targets A4 width.
- The `@media print` block overrides body padding to zero and delegates margins to the `@page` rule — this is the correct pattern for `html-pdf-node` (which uses a headless Chromium print pipeline).
- Section comments (`/* Header */`, `/* Experience */`, etc.) make the CSS maintainable.
- The flexbox-based `.job-header` (title left, dates right) will render correctly in Chromium.
- The `.competencies` flex-wrap pill design is clean and ATS-safe (plain text content inside spans, no images).

---

### Issues Found

#### Suggestion (nice to have) — No file-level comment

The plan does not require a JSDoc header, and for a tiny single-export utility this is a low bar. However, since the file is the single source of truth for PDF generation and will be imported from two different places (Tasks 3 and 4), a one-line comment stating its purpose would improve discoverability:

```typescript
/** Returns a complete self-contained HTML document used for PDF generation. */
export function getCvHtml(): string {
```

This is a suggestion only, not a requirement.

#### Suggestion (nice to have) — LinkedIn URL trailing slash inconsistency

The LinkedIn `href` attribute is `https://linkedin.com/in/andrii-popenko-3331b3193/` (trailing slash) but the visible link text is `linkedin.com/in/andrii-popenko-3331b3193` (no trailing slash). This is cosmetically inconsistent — the text and href should agree. The cv.md version in Task 1 uses the full URL with trailing slash for both. This has no functional impact on the PDF.

Location: `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/cv-astro/src/utils/cv-html.ts`, line 114.

---

### What Was Done Well

- The implementation is a clean, zero-dependency template utility — exactly the right architectural choice for this use case.
- HTML entities are used consistently and correctly throughout (`&`, `–`, `—`, `·`, `’`). This is significantly better than using raw Unicode dashes and apostrophes, which can cause encoding issues in some PDF renderers.
- CSS section grouping with comments makes the stylesheet readable despite being embedded in a template string.
- The condensed single-paragraph format for the older/shorter roles (Hasky, Mamontova Studio, SSTC NRS, teaching roles) is the right typographic choice for a single-page PDF — it matches professional CV conventions.
- The `@page` rule is correctly placed inside `@media print` and correctly specifies `size: A4 portrait`, which will interact correctly with `html-pdf-node`'s `{ format: "A4" }` option defined in Task 4.
- The function is ready to be imported with zero changes needed for Tasks 3 and 4.
